items.title: Trump tells BBC Netanyahu did not defy him
items.summary: In a call with the US president, the BBC’s Sarah Smith asked Trump about the war in Iran and his relationship with the Israeli leader.

items.title: Israel and Iran flare-up could strengthen Tehran's negotiating hand
items.summary: Iran appears emboldened by the outcome and its leaders may sense Trump's appetite for risk is low.

items.title: Hundreds of aftershocks jolt Philippines as officials say death toll could rise
items.summary: Dozens of people are dead and hundreds more injured following an earthquake in the country's south.

items.title: Trump booed in New York as he becomes first US president to attend NBA Finals
items.summary: The catcalls came after ticketholders faced airport-style security to enter the venue at Madison Square Garden.

items.title: ICC suspends top prosecutor after investigating misconduct allegations
items.summary: Karim Khan denies all allegations of sexual misconduct and his lawyers say he rejects the decision in the strongest terms.

items.title: Man accused of killing mother-in-law with poison-laced satay
items.summary: Indonesia police allege the man killed the woman as he felt disrespected by her.

items.title: Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un vow stronger ties as North Korea visit wraps up
items.summary: Xi has wrapped up a two-day visit to Pyongyang, his first official trip to North Korea since 2019.

items.title: Tickets for Iran fans revoked, says federation
items.summary: Iran's football federation says its allocation of tickets for the group stage of the World Cup has been revoked just days before the tournament.

items.title: Kenyan police fire tear gas at protest against US Ebola quarantine centre plan
items.summary: Protesters are concerned about cross-border infection risks and the lack of transparency from the government about the treatment centre.

items.title: Black bear caught in Japan after days of sightings
items.summary: The capture in Utsunomiya comes as bear attacks have reached record levels across Japan.

items.title: All 24 Indian crew rescued from tanker set ablaze off Oman after US strike
items.summary: Crew members of the unladen tanker had sent distress messages saying the vessel was on fire and sinking.

items.title: Stock market jitters remain amid tech fears and renewed Middle East attacks
items.summary: Markets in Asia are hit by a tech sell-off, and oil is volatile as Iran and Israel launch attacks on each other.

items.title: Eriksen at home and 'doing well' after collapse
items.summary: Christian Eriksen says he is "doing well" and is at home with his family following his collapse in Sunday's game against Ukraine.

items.title: Somali referee Artan barred from entering US
items.summary: Omar Artan, who was set to be the first Somali to referee at the World Cup finals, is dropped from the list of officials after he was denied entry to the United States.

items.title: SpaceX's stock market blast-off could be Musk's biggest gamble yet
items.summary: SpaceX is preparing for a stock market debut that could transform the company, the wider market and Elon Musk's fortune.

items.title: How one of India's most successful female politicians is losing her party
items.summary: Mamata Banerjee's once-dominant Trinamool Congress party is unravelling, weeks after losing power in West Bengal.

items.title: Iran's strike on Israel suggests the regime's sense of resilience is growing
items.summary: Iran's decision to risk jeopardising peace talks may reflect how its leaders view their current position, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

items.title: How Eriksen's heart device kicked into action
items.summary: Denmark's team doctor said an ICD implanted into the footballer's chest responded as it should after he collapsed on Sunday.

items.title: 'A World Cup for them not us': Fans' anger at US travel bans and visa restrictions
items.summary: Fans across the world say US travel bans and visa regulations make them feel excluded from the World Cup.

items.title: Friendship or leverage: Why is Xi Jinping in North Korea?
items.summary: Beijing is trying to reassert influence over a strategically vital yet deeply unpredictable partner.

items.title: 'We don't look at the sky any more': The Air India crash victims who were not on the plane
items.summary: A grandfather, a survivor, a witness: one year after the crash, the people on the ground tell their stories.

items.title: Spain's visitor numbers hit new highs as tourists avoid Middle East
items.summary: The European country had 9.1 million international visitors in April, the most ever for that month.

items.title: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
items.summary: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

items.title: Steve Rosenberg: Russia's economic forum overshadowed by drone attacks on St Petersburg
items.summary: The BBC's Russia editor saw Putin's flagship economic event overshadowed by Ukrainian drones attacks.

items.title: US truck carrying fireworks catches fire, sparking spectacular display
items.summary: No one was harmed in the incident, which saw motorists treated to an early Fourth of July show.

items.title: Watch: Southern Lights timelapse filmed from space
items.summary: This occurrence of aurora australis, or Southern Lights, was captured by Nasa astronaut Jessica Meir.

items.title: Has Trump lost control of the Iran war?
items.summary: For the first time since the start of a precarious ceasefire two months ago, Israel and Iran have traded missile strikes.

items.title: Schoolchildren run from collapsing roof as ground shakes in Philippines quake
items.summary: Pupils could be heard screaming as the earthquake struck the city of Digos, but the school said nobody was injured in the incident.

items.title: Iran's World Cup football team arrives in Mexico amid US visa row
items.summary: The players and staff will have to fly in and out of the US for each of their games in the group stage.
